name: S2: restore Subjects richness (status grouping, urgency sort, book count, animated skeleton)
description: SubjectsBrowse.tsx renders a flat unsorted list; buildSubjectsIndex in use-subjects-index.ts filters to status='active' only, making paused/archived subjects invisible. SubjectIndexItem omits status and urgencyBoostUntil fields. The legacy library.tsx had status grouping (active/paused/archived), urgency-boost sort (urgencyBoostUntil), book counts (books.length), and an animated loading skeleton. ShimmerSkeleton is available at apps/mobile/src/components/common/ShimmerSkeleton.tsx.
acceptanceCriteria:
1. SubjectIndexItem gains status (active|paused|archived) and urgencyBoostUntil (ISO date|null); buildSubjectsIndex passes all statuses through (removes the active-only filter).
2. SubjectsBrowse groups subjects into Active / Paused / Archived sections with headers; empty groups are omitted.
3. Within each group, subjects with a non-expired urgencyBoostUntil sort above non-urgent peers.
4. Each subject row shows books.length as a book count.
5. SubjectsBrowse accepts an isLoading prop and renders a ShimmerSkeleton-shaped placeholder when true, replacing the current plain spinner.
6. SubjectsBrowse.test.tsx covers status grouping, urgency sort, book count display, and skeleton loading state.
7. use-subjects-index.test.tsx (buildSubjectsIndex) covers the all-status pass-through and urgencyBoostUntil field mapping.
